parsed_review.summary: CP00-910 is a descriptor-only RP30 embedded-HRX bridge pack (10 units, risk A, RP30.P02.M03.S10-RP30.P02.M04.S06: 4 RecruitmentWorkflow tail + 6 RiskWorkflow slices). Read-only verification confirms the pack-level descriptor-verified claim is supported and that production_ready/runtime_ready correctly remain false pending the post-review closeout sequence. registry.js (HRX_CP910_PACK_BINDING, HRX_CP910_REQUIREMENTS, createHrxCp910RecruitmentRiskWorkflowBridgeDescriptor) is internally consistent and matches the baseline, manifest, plan, and regenerated contracts/hrx-people-contract.json exactly. validators.js enforces descriptor-only, no-write (HRX_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION), no-real-data, deferred runtime/decision/record/score, and authority-boundary checks (claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, Hermes emits_runtime_receipt=false, runtime_opening deferred to CP00-911). model.test.js gives meaningful CP910 coverage including the on-disk contract projection. The validation script chains all CP897-CP910 bindings; all referenced npm scripts exist; README documents the scope; unrelated dirty files are correctly excluded; readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON). Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not final approval authority; Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling, not product runtime. No P0/P1 findings. One non-blocking P3: command-evidence.json captures only the 6 HRX-specific commands (all exit 0); the full deterministic validation ladder and git diff --check are pending and cannot be re-executed under read-only review, so promotion remains contingent on those steps plus adjudication, commit, and human final approval for any enterprise-trust/runtime claim.

findings.title: Full closeout validation ladder not captured in evidence and not re-executable under read-only review
findings.details: command-evidence.json documents only six HRX-specific commands (hrx_syntax_check_initial, hrx_model_test, rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate), all exit_code 0, with hermes_gate.status=pending and gate_outcome=pending_pack_review. The broader closeout ladder required by the review baseline (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not captured in this evidence file, and as a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob only, no Bash) I cannot independently re-execute any command. This review's assurance is therefore based on static code/artifact consistency plus the recorded HRX command exit codes, not on independent re-execution. This is expected for the pre-promotion state: the pack transparently keeps production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false and its own notes state the full deterministic ladder, adjudication, queue regeneration, and commit are required before production_ready.
findings.recommendation: During the deterministic post-review closeout step, run and capture the full validation ladder and git diff --check before promoting production_ready, and ensure the manifest/adjudication/construction-inspection are updated from PENDING only after those pass. Retain human final approval for any enterprise-trust or runtime-opening claim (runtime opening deferred to CP00-911).
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking and by design for this pre-promotion state; does not block pack or this-CP goal closeout. Tracked as an evidence/provenance note: descriptor-only static verification is complete, full-ladder execution remains a deterministic closeout dependency outside this read-only receipt.
